content.text: CI Quality Gates for GenAI Application Pipelines

Continuous integration pipelines for GenAI applications must enforce checks that standard pipelines do not include. A prompt change that passes unit tests and linting can still degrade model behaviour, introduce a security vulnerability, or violate governance rules — none of which a standard build pipeline detects.

This document defines a six-gate CI pipeline for GenAI applications, explains the purpose and implementation of each gate, provides GitHub Actions YAML for the full pipeline, and covers failure handling, bypass procedures, and pipeline health metrics.

attrs.alt: CI Quality Gate Pipeline — six gates from schema lint through build and deploy

content.text: Why Standard CI Pipelines Are Insufficient
A standard CI pipeline checks that code compiles, unit tests pass, and linting rules are satisfied. For GenAI applications, these checks are necessary but not sufficient:
A change to a prompt YAML file can break model behaviour without changing any Python or TypeScript. Standard linting does not read prompt files.
A unit test with a mocked LLM response does not catch prompt regressions. Only running the eval suite against the live model catches these.
A commit that removes the safety baseline from a prompt passes all standard checks and ships a vulnerability. A prompt lint gate catches this.
Without a version check gate, prompts are silently modified without a version bump, breaking the audit trail.
Secrets in prompts are invisible to standard scanners.
A developer who embeds an API key directly in a prompt template bypasses standard secret-scanning tools that only scan source code files.

The CI pipeline is the last automated line of defence before a prompt change reaches production. Each gate must be cheap enough to run on every PR and precise enough to block the failure modes it was designed to catch.

Schema lint validates that every prompt YAML file and eval YAML file is syntactically valid and conforms to the expected schema. It runs first because it is fastest and its failure blocks all subsequent gates.
YAML syntax: valid YAML, no duplicate keys, no tab characters
Required fields present: id, version, model, max_tokens, system, evals
Field types correct: version is a semver string, max_tokens is an integer, evals is a list
No forbidden top-level keys (catches copy-paste errors from different template versions)
Eval cases have required fields: id, user_message, expected_keywords or expected_schema
Prompt lint applies semantic rules to the content of prompt templates. Unlike schema lint, it understands what the prompts say, not just their structure.
Safety baseline missing or below minimum version
Possible API key found in prompt body
User input wrapped in XML delimiter tags
User input not delimited — injection risk
No bare {{variable}} interpolation without XML wrapper
Undelimited variable may allow prompt injection
System section has 5 required parts (Role, Scope, Tone, Safety, Context)
No instruction to ignore previous instructions
max_tokens may be oversized for task type

# scripts/lint_prompts.py
import re, sys, yaml, pathlib

SAFETY_BASELINE_PATTERN = re.compile(
    r"safety baseline v(\d+)\.(\d+)", re.IGNORECASE
)
API_KEY_PATTERN = re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}")
BARE_VARIABLE = re.compile(r"\{\{[^}]+\}\}")
XML_WRAPPED   = re.compile(r"<[a-z_]+>\s*\{\{[^}]+\}\}\s*</[a-z_]+>")

def check_file(path):
    errs = []
    data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
    system = data.get("system", "")

    # PL-001: safety baseline
    m = SAFETY_BASELINE_PATTERN.search(system)
    if not m or (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))) < (2, 1):
        errs.append("PL-001: Safety baseline missing or below v2.1")

    # PL-002: API key in prompt body
    if API_KEY_PATTERN.search(system):
        errs.append("PL-002: Possible API key in prompt body")

    # PL-004: bare variable without XML wrapper
    bare = BARE_VARIABLE.findall(system)
    wrapped = XML_WRAPPED.findall(system)
    unwrapped = [v for v in bare if not any(v in w for w in wrapped)]
    if unwrapped:
        errs.append(f"PL-004: Undelimited variables: {unwrapped}")

    return errs

all_errors = []
for path in pathlib.Path("prompts/").glob("**/*.yaml"):
    errors = check_file(path)
    for e in errors:
        all_errors.append(f"{path}: {e}")
        print(f"::error file={path}::{e}")

sys.exit(1 if all_errors else 0)

The version check gate enforces the semantic versioning discipline defined in the prompt governance policy. Any change to a prompt's system text, variables, model, or max_tokens must be accompanied by an appropriate version bump.
Callers must update to pass/remove the variable
May change model behaviour; evals must re-run

The eval suite runs the changed prompt templates against the live Anthropic API and checks whether the model's outputs meet the pass/fail thresholds defined in the eval cases. This is the most expensive gate and the most important: it is the only gate that verifies that the prompt change produces acceptable model behaviour.
May be raised to 90%/95% for HIGH/CRITICAL risk; never lowered
No waiver; any failure blocks merge unconditionally
If 80–85%, requires tech lead approval to merge
No waiver; a regression case failure means a known bug has reappeared

Running the full eval suite on every PR is expensive. The gate should run evals only for templates that changed, and use SHA-256 caching to skip templates whose content has not changed since the last run.
The security scan gate runs two checks: a secrets scanner that finds hardcoded credentials in any changed file, and an injection surface scan that flags prompts missing the required input delimiters.
Standard secret scanning tools (Gitleaks, Trufflehog) do not know to look inside YAML prompt files. The CI configuration must explicitly include prompt directories in the scan scope.

# .gitleaks.toml — extend default rules to cover prompt YAML files
[extend]
useDefault = true

[[rules]]
id          = "anthropic-api-key-in-prompt"
description = "Anthropic API key embedded in prompt template"
regex       = '''sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{95}'''
path        = '''prompts/.*\.yaml'''
severity    = "CRITICAL"
tags        = ["anthropic", "prompt-injection-surface"]

[[rules]]
id          = "openai-api-key-in-prompt"
description = "OpenAI API key in prompt template"
regex       = '''sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48}'''
path        = '''prompts/.*\.yaml'''
severity    = "CRITICAL"

# Never allow baseline to be overridden by a secrets finding
[allowlist]
paths       = ["tests/fixtures/", "docs/examples/"]
The final gate builds the application artefact and runs a smoke test against the staging environment to confirm the LLM integration path is wired correctly end-to-end.
Send one request through the full LLM path in staging (not mocked) and assert the response is non-empty
Verify the response stop_reason is end_turn (not max_tokens, which indicates the prompt is producing truncated output)
Check the OTel span is emitted with the correct llm.provider and llm.model attributes
Verify the cost metric is emitted (catches misconfigured token tracking)
Assert the feature flag is present and defaults to enabled

# tests/smoke/test_llm_integration.py
import os
import anthropic
import pytest

@pytest.fixture
def client():
    return anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])

def test_summarise_smoke(client):
    """Smoke test: real API call in staging, checks structure not content."""
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",  # cheapest model for smoke
        max_tokens=128,
        system="Summarise the following text in one sentence.",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "<text>The sky is blue.</text>"}],
    )
    assert response.content, "Empty response from LLM"
    assert response.stop_reason == "end_turn", (
        f"Unexpected stop_reason: {response.stop_reason}. "
        "Prompt may be producing truncated output."
    )
    text = response.content[0].text
    assert len(text) > 5, "Response too short to be a valid summary"

def test_feature_flag_present():
    """Confirm the LLM kill-switch flag exists in the flag service."""
    from your_flag_client import flags
    assert flags.flag_exists("llm_summarise"), (
        "Feature flag 'llm_summarise' not found in flag service. "
        "Add it before deploying to production."
    )
The complete pipeline runs gates 1–3 in parallel, waits for all to pass, then runs gate 4 (eval), then runs gates 5–6 in parallel. Total wall-clock time is typically 7–15 minutes.

# .github/workflows/ci-genai.yml
name: GenAI CI Quality Gates

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'prompts/**'
      - 'src/**'
      - 'tests/**'

jobs:
  # ── Gates 1-3 run in parallel ──────────────────────────────────────────
  schema-lint:
    name: "Gate 1 — Schema Lint"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install pyyaml jsonschema
      - run: python scripts/lint_schema.py

  prompt-lint:
    name: "Gate 2 — Prompt Lint"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install pyyaml
      - run: python scripts/lint_prompts.py

  version-check:
    name: "Gate 3 — Version Check"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install pyyaml semver
      - run: python scripts/check_versions.py

  # ── Gate 4: Eval suite (only after 1-3 pass) ──────────────────────────
  eval-suite:
    name: "Gate 4 — Eval Suite"
    needs: [schema-lint, prompt-lint, version-check]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install anthropic pyyaml
      - name: Restore eval cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: .eval_cache/
          key: eval-cache-${{ hashFiles('prompts/**') }}
          restore-keys: eval-cache-
      - run: python scripts/run_evals_ci.py
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

  # ── Gates 5-6 run in parallel after Gate 4 ────────────────────────────
  security-scan:
    name: "Gate 5 — Security Scan"
    needs: eval-suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
      - name: Run Gitleaks
        uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install pyyaml
      - run: python scripts/scan_injection_surface.py

  build-and-smoke:
    name: "Gate 6 — Build & Smoke Test"
    needs: eval-suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: python -m pytest tests/smoke/ -v
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_STAGING }}
          ENVIRONMENT: staging
No CI gate should be permanently bypassable, but the team must have a documented exception process for genuine emergencies (production hotfixes, security patches).
Fix the lint failure before merging
Run full eval within 24h; revert if fails
Scan must pass within 4h of merge
Fix failing test within next PR

# Branch protection rule — prevents bypass without documented approval
# Set in GitHub repository settings (or via Terraform):
# Required status checks:
#   - Gate 1 — Schema Lint
#   - Gate 2 — Prompt Lint
#   - Gate 3 — Version Check
#   - Gate 4 — Eval Suite
#   - Gate 5 — Security Scan
#   - Gate 6 — Build & Smoke Test
# Require branches to be up to date before merging: true
# Require approvals: 1 (domain reviewer)
# Dismiss stale reviews on push: true
# Restrict who can push to matching branches: tech-leads team
A CI pipeline is only useful if it stays fast and reliable. Gate failures due to flakiness desensitise the team; slow gates get bypassed. Track these metrics weekly:
Profile which file is slow; cache pip installs
Reduce eval cases or increase parallelism
Identify flaky eval and stabilise or remove
If higher, investigate whether gate thresholds are too strict
Time from PR open to gate results
Optimise parallelism; avoid sequential gates unnecessarily

# scripts/collect_pipeline_metrics.py
# Reads GitHub Actions run history and reports gate health metrics.
import os, statistics
from github import Github  # pip install PyGithub

g      = Github(os.environ["GITHUB_TOKEN"])
repo   = g.get_repo(os.environ["GITHUB_REPOSITORY"])
runs   = list(repo.get_workflow_runs(workflow_id="ci-genai.yml", status="completed"))[:200]

durations   = []
failures    = {"schema-lint": 0, "prompt-lint": 0, "version-check": 0,
               "eval-suite": 0, "security-scan": 0, "build-and-smoke": 0}
total_runs  = len(runs)

for run in runs:
    dur = (run.updated_at - run.created_at).total_seconds()
    durations.append(dur)
    for job in run.jobs():
        if job.conclusion == "failure" and job.name.split("—")[0].strip().lower() in failures:
            key = job.name.split("—")[0].strip().lower().replace(" ", "-")
            if key in failures:
                failures[key] += 1

p50 = statistics.median(durations) / 60
p95 = sorted(durations)[int(len(durations)*0.95)] / 60

print(f"Pipeline p50 duration: {p50:.1f} min")
print(f"Pipeline p95 duration: {p95:.1f} min")
print(f"Gate failure rates (last {total_runs} runs):")
for gate, count in failures.items():
    print(f"  {gate}: {count/total_runs*100:.1f}%")

A six-gate CI pipeline enforces the quality bar that standard pipelines cannot: schema validity, prompt governance rules, version discipline, eval pass rates, secret scanning, and end-to-end smoke tests
Gates 1–3 run in parallel and complete in ~30 seconds; gate 4 (eval) is the most expensive gate at 5–10 minutes and gates 5–6 run in parallel after it
The eval gate is the only gate that catches prompt regressions — it must run against the live model, not a mock
Adversarial eval cases require 100% pass rate with no waiver; this is the unconditional floor for safety
Eval caching by content hash reduces gate 4 cost significantly on PRs that change non-prompt code
Pipeline bypasses must be documented, require named approvals, and trigger a mandatory follow-up within 24h
Gate health metrics (duration, flakiness, bypass frequency) must be reviewed weekly to keep the pipeline trusted
